title: stellularia graminea var. uliginosa
intro: Stellularia graminea var. uliginosa, also known as the marsh stellularia or marsh blue devil, is a plant species native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, where its leaves have been found to exhibit medicinal properties due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Stellularia graminea var. uliginosa include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
